A message from Richard Branson
[https://images.usw2.cordial.com/1466/1200x1444/Frame-2-5.png]
https://e.mk.virginvoyages.com/c2/1466:68e91498f20b6fdc220d2036:ot:6891da094489de52ba02d2d7:1/3e43a64c?jwtH=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9&&jwtS=BtV4j4HdOpi0_F4zx0WtsjOznUSKkpMwSWKaH0CwhOg

 

Dear future Sailor,

As we stood on Brilliant Lady's deck, sailing past Lady Liberty into New York
Harbor, I couldn't help but think about you — those who've shown interest in
what we're building but haven't yet stepped aboard.

You've been watching. You've been curious. And now, with our fourth kid-free
ship joining the fleet, this brilliant moment feels like the perfect time to
say: your adventure is ready when you are.

During our NYC arrival, 2,400 voices — passengers and crew — joined together to
sing Queen's "Don't Stop Me Now" as Manhattan welcomed us home. We chose that
song because we're just getting started. As our friend Astrid Jorgensen from Pub
Choir said, "When we sing alone, we may not sound like a star, but when we sing
together, it sounds brilliant."

That's exactly what Virgin Voyages is about: coming together, celebrating life,
and creating moments that matter. Four magnificent Lady Ships where you can be
brilliantly, authentically yourself, while connecting with like-minded people.

At a time when the world can sometimes feel less joyful, we're here to remind
you that being with those we love — that's where joy lives. We have a simple
philosophy at Virgin: be the reason someone smiles today.

This isn't the end of our story - it's the next brilliant chapter, and we're
just getting started.

Ready to see what all the fuss is about?

With endless optimism,
Richard







 

Richard Branson
